text: Energy Pedals II is a harder iteration of Energy Pedals I. You will be given the same format and field of view as the first exercise. However, you will require much more coordination with the left and right arm and their corresponding foot pedals. You can see that the object presented to you in the field of view has three parts to it, two of the barbell portions and a center sphere. You will be required to perform bipolar coagulation on the two barbell objects while either performing monopolar coag or cut, depending upon the instructions on the sphere.

The location of the objects are presented at random, and the orientation of the object once it reaches its final destination also varies. This will force you to use your wrist articulation skills to best orient your grasper to grab the barbell portions and position the tips of your scissors so as they are only touching the sphere when applying electrocautery. Once you start using the electrocautery, you'll see the object start to smoke, and eventually it'll turn green. When it does that, you can release the foot pedal and apply cautery to the next part of the object.

As mentioned in energy pedals one, we want to draw attention to the interface, which allows you to orient yourself with your foot position and which pedal you are hovering over. At this moment, the interface is indicating that my right foot is over the left coagulation pedal, and I am now pressing it. Subsequently, you can see the interface shift to indicate that I'm now pressing the right coagulation pedal. It may take a few tries to get comfortable adapting to the interface and using the right foot pedals, but ultimately that is the goal of this exercise. Looking down to see where the location of your foot is in relation to the foot pedals is suboptimal. Penalties on this exercise can be incurred when over-quaterizing an area, pressing the wrong foot pedal, and instrument collisions.
